name: Alltrust
description_short: Alltrust is a not-for-profit credit union serving Massachusetts with checking, savings, loans, and financial planning services.
description_long:
Alltrust Credit Union is a community-focused, not-for-profit financial institution based in Massachusetts. The organization operates multiple physical locations in the South Shore region, including branches in Fairhaven, Fall River, and New Bedford. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Alltrust emphasizes member ownership and reinvesting profits back into the organization and local community.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of consumer banking products and services. These include checking and savings accounts with high-yield Kasasa options, auto loans, personal loans, home equity loans and lines of credit, debit and credit cards, and safe deposit boxes. Members can access their accounts through mobile banking and online e-services for check deposits, bill payments, and fund transfers. The organization also provides financial planning services in partnership with Olive Tree Wealth and LPL Financial, though these are offered by separate affiliated entities.

Alltrust distinguishes itself through member-centric programs and community involvement. The debit card round-up savings program automatically transfers rounded-up purchase amounts into member savings accounts. The Skip-A-Payment Program allows members to defer one consumer loan payment per year under certain conditions. As a community credit union, Alltrust actively supports local events, organizations, and provides annual scholarships to high school students in its service area.

Alltrust is best suited for Massachusetts residents in its service territory who value community banking and member-owned financial institutions. However, the organization's geographic footprint appears limited to South Shore Massachusetts, which restricts accessibility for those outside this region. The website provides limited detail on specific APR rates, loan terms, or account fee structures, requiring prospective members to contact the credit union directly for detailed product information. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts with Kasasa high-yield options
pros: Not-for-profit structure means profits are reinvested in members and community rather than shareholders
Free mobile banking and e-services including mobile check deposit and bill payment
Debit card round-up savings program automatically builds savings from everyday purchases
Skip-A-Payment Program allows deferring one consumer loan payment annually for qualifying loans
High-yield Kasasa checking and savings accounts available
Multiple physical branch locations in Fairhaven, Fall River, and New Bedford with safe deposit boxes
Financial planning services available through partnership with LPL Financial advisors
cons: Geographic limitation: Service area appears restricted to Massachusetts South Shore region, excluding most potential members
Limited online transparency: Website does not display specific APR rates, fees, or loan terms requiring direct contact
Skip-A-Payment Program has significant restrictions: only one payment per year, excludes credit cards and mortgages, and doesn't reduce interest accrual
best_for: Massachusetts residents in the South Shore area (Fairhaven, Fall River, New Bedford region) seeking community banking
Credit union members who prioritize not-for-profit institutions and local community support
Consumers interested in automatic savings programs and financial planning guidance
diagnosis: Alltrust is best for Massachusetts residents within its South Shore service territory who prefer community-based, not-for-profit banking with innovative savings programs like debit card round-ups. The main caveat is geographic restriction—the credit union is inaccessible to those outside its limited Massachusetts footprint, and the website lacks transparent pricing information, requiring direct contact for APR rates and specific terms.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
